source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/01_HAD1717MC, MG/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/10_Performance/EN 62220-1-1 DQE Test report rev1 20200310.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: DQE test report for HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC digital flat panel X-ray detectors tested using EN 62220-1-1.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports performance and regulatory documentation for digital flat panel X-ray detector characteristics including DQE, MTF, NPS, linearity, lag, defects, frame rate, and recovery time.
keyPoints: Report No. A-PR-0-001 Rev.1 was approved on Mar/10/2020.
The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
Testing includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The device is described as a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
The active area is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm with 3052 x 3052 active pixels.
Measured DQE values are reported for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp/mm.

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: The detective quantum efficiency (DQE) was evaluated as a function of spatial frequency over a wide range of incident exposure levels.

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

concepts.name: Noise Power Spectrum
concepts.evidence: The NPS is the noise power spectrum measured at same air kerma.

concepts.name: Static X-ray detector
concepts.evidence: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: Scintillator
concepts.evidence: There are two types of scintillator which are CsI and Gadox.

concepts.name: Active area
concepts.evidence: The active area of this device is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

concepts.name: Pixel defect criteria
concepts.evidence: Pixel Defect (Cluster) -Criteria : Normal within the criteria

evidence.claim: The document is EN 62220-1-1 DQE test report Doc. No. A-PR-0-001 Rev.1.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 DQE test report Doc. No. A-PR-0-001 Rev. No. 1

evidence.claim: The report applies to HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MG, HAD1717MC

evidence.claim: Revision 1 is dated Mar/10/2020 and changed the predicate device.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. Rev. date Revision contents Remark 0 Sep/20/2019 Establishment 1 Mar/10/2020 Change of predicate device

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Mar/10/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Kim, Ki Hyun Mar/10/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong Jin Mar/10/2020 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Mar/10/2020

evidence.claim: The test report includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: The listed standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determining DQE of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector active pixel area is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm

evidence.claim: The detector has 3052 x 3052 active area pixels, totaling 9,314,704.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 3052 x 3052 = 9,314,704

evidence.claim: The device frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate • 1 fps

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The X-ray absorber is a scintillator with CsI and Gadox types.
evidence.evidenceText: There are two types of scintillator which are CsI and Gadox.

evidence.claim: The absorber wavelength is 540 nm in the visible region.
evidence.evidenceText: This absorbers wavelength is 540nm(Visible region).

evidence.claim: Image data is transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 16-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two-dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC measured DQE values are 70.10% at 0.1 lp, 57.40% at 0.5 lp, 52.10% at 1 lp, 38% at 2 lp, and 28.40% at 3 lp.
HAD1717MG measured DQE values are 42.40% at 0.1 lp, 33.20% at 0.5 lp, 27.50% at 1 lp, 15.60% at 2 lp, and 9.45% at 3 lp.
Aliasing artifacts are described as negligible because MTF near the sampling cut-off frequency is approximately close to zero.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector element determines the spatial resolution capability of the detector. Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm. The MTF value at this frequency is approximately close to zero.

evidence.claim: No under-scanning is possible and the full active area is used.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The report references alignment and collimation requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.
evidence.evidenceText: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 2 seconds, and image readout, transferring, and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 2 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure. Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: Stability time is 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Stability time : 5 sec.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC meets regulatory standards outlined in safety report DRMCBL1909-0016 and EMC report DREKCEE1905-0342.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC meets IEC standards outlined in safety report DRMCBL1909-0016 and EMC report DREKCEE1905-0342.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the IEC standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: Linearity and sensitivity are reported as 152.47 LSB/uGy for HAD1717MC and 70.894 LSB/uGy for HAD1717MG.
evidence.pageTarget: HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC Linearity and Sensitivity

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal metadata says FDA, but the source text does not explicitly name FDA; it only references 21CFR1020.31 or .32.
Regulatory approvals and IEC compliance reference separate safety and EMC reports, but the exact regulatory and IEC standards in those reports are not listed in this source text.
Some OCR text contains artifacts such as 'menstioned', 'Phot-Diode', '3..0 lp/mm', formula formatting loss, and repeated Korean '변경된 필드 코드'.
